meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: You overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a functional SaaS that handles real-world complexity. By integrating a professional pricing model—tiers, feature gating, and Stripe checkout—you demonstrate engineering maturity. This checklist guides you through building a production-grade pricing engine using Antigravity and Claude Code, transforming a basic project into a hireable asset.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of engineering careers. You’ve spent months grinding tutorials, yet you have nothing to show that commands respect. Employers don't care about your 'To-Do list' app; they care about how you handle the messy, transactional reality of a real product.

The secret isn't more code; it's commercial architecture. By building a robust SaaS pricing engine, you shift from 'student' to 'builder.' This guide leverages Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, turning your first project into a showcase of professional-grade logic.

steps.title: Define Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data structure for Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This forces you to think about database schema design and feature permissions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS application with tiered pricing (Free, Pro, Enterprise) including feature flags and subscription status.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe API to handle real-world payment flows. This is the gold standard for proving you can handle external third-party integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a boilerplate for a Stripe Checkout session handler in Next.js that maps user tiers to Stripe price IDs.

steps.title: Build Feature Gating Logic
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that restricts access based on the user's subscription tier. This is where you demonstrate security-first coding habits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Auth middleware function that checks the user's subscription status from the database and gates access to specific API routes.

steps.title: Setup Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Handle asynchronous events like subscription cancellations or successful payments. This proves you understand backend reliability and event-driven architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure webhook handler for Stripe events to update the user subscription status in the database.

steps.title: Finalize Pricing UI Components
steps.description: Build a responsive pricing table that highlights the 'Pro' tier. Aesthetics matter as much as logic when demonstrating a product-ready mindset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Shadcn UI pricing table component with toggleable monthly and yearly billing cycles.

pro_tips: Always implement 'Yearly vs Monthly' toggles; it shows recruiters you understand revenue retention strategies.
Use mock data factories for your pricing tests to ensure your UI handles edge cases like currency symbol changes.
Treat your feature flags as constants in a separate file to keep your business logic clean and decoupled.
Log every failed payment attempt in your database; monitoring is a trait of a senior-level mindset.
Document your Stripe integration flow in a README file; recruiters read documentation before they touch your code.
content.next_steps: Once the pricing engine is live, deploy your app to Vercel and create a 60-second Loom video walking through the 'Pro' signup flow to attach to your LinkedIn profile.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Your portfolio is your leverage, and your first SaaS project is the key to unlocking your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
